text: Planning a trip is always an exciting part of the experience. Before I visited Japan, I spent weeks researching destinations, booking flights, and learning a few basic phrases. When I finally arrived, everything felt new and vibrant. The food was incredible—I tried sushi, ramen, and local street snacks that I had never even heard of. However, language barriers sometimes made communication difficult; a smile and a map often helped more than words. Despite these challenges, I learned so much about their traditions and daily life. Those memories—tasting fresh sushi at a small market or getting lost in a quiet temple garden—will stay with me forever.
